h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Okay, so `city` is the field name, and `Chicago` is the answer in that field for this row.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to systematically apply the header-value distinction to each column in sequence.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Use the colon as the check. Left side comes from the header row. Right side comes from this row’s cell. Cover `72`: `temperature` still names the field. Uncover it: `72` is the value.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a physical covering/uncovering heuristic to address the learner's persistent anxiety about swapping the header and value.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Use the colon as the check. Left side comes from the header row. Right side comes from this row’s cell. Cover `72`: `temperature` still names the field. Uncover it: `72` is the value.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor introduces a physical covering/uncovering heuristic to address the learner's persistent anxiety about swapping the header and value.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Use the colon as the check. Left side comes from the header row. Right side comes from this row’s cell. Cover `72`: `temperature` still names the field. Uncover it: `72` is the value.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor introduces a physical covering/uncovering heuristic to address the learner's persistent anxiety about swapping the header and value.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, unit can come from the scale marks.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to search only for parentheses or brackets to identify the y-axis unit.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: For y unit: a `$` printed on the y-axis scale counts as dollars. If there is no `$`, parentheses, or bracketed unit on the y-axis, enter `none`.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor expands the rule definition to accommodate the learner's observation of the dollar sign on the scale.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn exists to trigger contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn exists to trigger contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: For y unit: a `$` printed on the y-axis scale counts as dollars. If there is no `$`, parentheses, or bracketed unit on the y-axis, enter `none`.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor expands the rule definition to accommodate the learner's observation of the dollar sign on the scale.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: For y unit: a `$` printed on the y-axis scale counts as dollars. If there is no `$`, parentheses, or bracketed unit on the y-axis, enter `none`.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor expands the rule definition to accommodate the learner's observation of the dollar sign on the scale.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: The key strip under it helps: rabbit is the food, so rabbit to hawk, point on hawk.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to struggle with the visual distraction of the chase pose while trying to apply the arrow-key rule.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: [slides the key strip down until it covers the chase pose and leaves only the two labels showing]
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor physically covers the distracting chase pose with the key strip to help the learner focus purely on the labels and overcome their visual bias.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: [slides the key strip down until it covers the chase pose and leaves only the two labels showing]
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor physically covers the distracting chase pose with the key strip to help the learner focus purely on the labels and overcome their visual bias.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: [slides the key strip down until it covers the chase pose and leaves only the two labels showing]
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor physically covers the distracting chase pose with the key strip to help the learner focus purely on the labels and overcome their visual bias.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to place the remaining force cards on the diagram as prompted by the tutor.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor does not change strategy because the learner proceeds smoothly without any breakdown or resistance.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor does not change strategy because the learner proceeds smoothly without any breakdown or resistance.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor does not change strategy because the learner proceeds smoothly without any breakdown or resistance.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to systematically verify the elevation at different points on the contour line.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The arrow moved; the contour did not. Same printed line, same tile. Touch one new spot on the bend and read the legend number once more.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a physical manipulation of the overlay to address the learner's persistent intuition that the curve represents a route.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn occurs, so there is no contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn occurs, so there is no contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The arrow moved; the contour did not. Same printed line, same tile. Touch one new spot on the bend and read the legend number once more.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor introduces a physical manipulation of the overlay to address the learner's persistent intuition that the curve represents a route.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The arrow moved; the contour did not. Same printed line, same tile. Touch one new spot on the bend and read the legend number once more.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor introduces a physical manipulation of the overlay to address the learner's persistent intuition that the curve represents a route.
